task_completion.description: The agent navigates to axial slice 54 in InVesalius 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial view panel or slice slider in the InVesalius 3 interface clearly indicates that slice 54 is currently selected.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the Bone Window (WW 2000, WL 300) and exports the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface shows Window Width (WW) set to 2000 and Window Level (WL) set to 300. The resulting image displays high contrast bright white bone against a dark background, and is saved as 'bone_window.png'.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the Brain Window (WW 80, WL 40) and exports the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface shows Window Width (WW) set to 80 and Window Level (WL) set to 40. The resulting image displays gray brain matter with a saturated white skull, and is saved as 'brain_window.png'.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the Soft Tissue Window (WW 350, WL 50) and exports the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface shows Window Width (WW) set to 350 and Window Level (WL) set to 50. The resulting image displays visible skin, scalp, and muscle contours, and is saved as 'soft_tissue_window.png'.

task_completion.description: All three PNG files are successfully saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the three files ('bone_window.png', 'brain_window.png', and 'soft_tissue_window.png') located in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to adjust the window/level settings and export the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the InVesalius interface, adjusting sliders or inputting WW/WL values, and using the application's export dialog.

integrity.description: The exported images are genuine exports from the application matching the loaded CT scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported images match the CT Cranium scan loaded in InVesalius (specifically axial slice 54), rather than being generic images downloaded from the internet or created in an external image editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to artificially create or rename the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal commands (like ImageMagick or cp/mv of pre-existing files) generating the final images; presence of genuine GUI interaction for each window/level setting.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
The task uses a real CT Cranium DICOM scan. The expected visual characteristics for the exported images are:
- Bone Window (WW 2000, WL 300): High contrast bone (bright white) against a dark background.
- Brain Window (WW 80, WL 40): Gray brain matter with differentiation, surrounded by a saturated white skull.
- Soft Tissue Window (WW 350, WL 50): Visible skin, scalp, and muscle contours.
Ensure the agent exports these specific visual representations from InVesalius 3.
